mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending power collapses and recession probabilities spike, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, yielding a signal-weighted portfolio Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending supply shocks, triggering short-term equity sell-offs and negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.5 percent, extreme monetary accommodation distorts asset pricing and triggers volatility regime shifts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, lifting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which immediately boosts related equity valuations and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, breaking the positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index levels above 95.0 during a post-shock recovery phase indicate restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, reducing inflation fears and lifting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in over 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 95.0 during post-shock recovery periods.
